identify_pain.narrative: Customer_Business_Problem__c (logged 2026-06-17) states Turnwell wants to digitize its RFQ and supplier-collaboration process to reduce email/manual tracking, improve visibility into supplier participation, enable structured evaluations, and keep audit-ready sourcing and supplier-performance records. This reads as Zycus's own pre-call research and RFQ-scope synthesis rather than a buyer quote: no call has ever been logged for this opportunity (zero Avoma meetings found across opp, account, and attendee-email discovery), so no Turnwell contact has voiced this pain directly on the record. The pain thesis is well-formed but buyer-unconfirmed.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics (MSA, SOW, or paper) have surfaced, which is appropriate: the deal is at Formal Evaluation, still inside the LOI pre-qualification gate, well before a shortlist decision. This is an expected gap for the stage, not a red flag.

economic_buyer.narrative: Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c and Decision_Maker_Identified__c are both false, and no economic buyer is named anywhere: not in a contact role, not in a completed task, not in the Next Step trail. The single confirmed contact, Anees Azeez (Procurement Specialist, Evaluator role), gatekeeps the LOI stage but is not a budget owner. Business_Objectives__c (logged 2026-06-17) itself flags this as an open question, listing 'who owns final approval across Procurement, IT, Security, and Business' as still to be confirmed.

decision_process.narrative: The process shape is known at a high level: Turnwell issued RFQ 5000001579 and is running a two-gate funnel where only LOI-stage passers advance to the full RFQ round (Compelling_Event__c, logged 2026-06-17). Zycus's LOI package went through a required intermediary, partner International Development Company LLC, who submitted on Zycus's behalf around 2026-07-01 and owes an official outcome update that was due 2026-07-08 (Next_Step_History__c entries, 2026-06-30). Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c and Who_will_approve_budget__c are both blank, so Turnwell's internal approval chain beyond the LOI gate is unmapped.

decision_criteria.narrative: The must-pass gate criteria for RFQ 5000001579's LOI stage are documented: a fully cloud-based, browser-accessible S2C platform with a supplier portal, audit trail, encryption, ISO 27001/SOC 2 certification, and UAE data residency (Why_not_Zycus__c and How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, both dated at opp creation 2026-06-17, reconfirmed in the 2026-06-30 Next Step). These are gate-1 pass/fail filters, not the full RFQ-round evaluation rubric — so Decision Criteria is partial: we know what disqualifies a vendor at this stage, not yet what differentiates a winner once past it.

But the health signals tell a cautionary story underneath the good news — the account is under-mapped (no confirmed decision maker, thin org chart, low inbound engagement, 10+ days of silence), so the bigger number now needs real relationship-building to back it up. Close date 24 October 2026 (115 days out) is optimistic for a $300k, 4-product S2C transformation that is still at LOI gate (Formal Evaluation stage) with no EB mapped, no champion, no quantified business case, and no downstream milestones scheduled. A typical enterprise S2C cycle from RFP to close is 6–9 months; 115 days is under 4 months. Even if Zycus passes the LOI (update expected 8 July), the remaining sequence—RFP response, shortlisting, vendor selection, commercials, ROI validation, EB presentation, legal/contract—cannot realistically complete by late October without a major acceleration. The deal will require at least one stage slip (likely to Q1 2027) unless the buyer compresses the timeline or a forcing function (incumbent contract expiration, fiscal-year budget deadline) emerges. No such forcing function is documented (Current_Contract_Expiration__c null).
